question_text:
Read the following passage and answer the questions:
Metals in the middle of the activity series, such as iron, zinc, lead, etc., are usually present as sulphides or carbonates in nature. It is easier to obtain a metal from its oxide, as compared to its sulphides and carbonates. Therefore, prior to reduction, the metal sulphides and carbonates must be converted into metal oxides. Sulphide ores are converted into oxides by heating strongly in the presence of excess air (roasting). Carbonate ores are changed into oxides by heating strongly in limited air (calcination).
(a) What is the difference between a mineral and an ore?
(b) Why is calcination carried out for carbonate ores instead of roasting?
(c) (i) Give an example of a metal that can be extracted by reducing its oxide with carbon and write the corresponding chemical reaction.
OR
(c) (ii) Highly reactive metals like Sodium (Na) cannot be obtained from their oxides by reduction with carbon. Why?

question_text:

Gregor Mendel conducted hybridization experiments on garden peas for seven years and proposed the laws of inheritance in living organisms. In one of his experiments, known as a monohybrid cross, he crossed tall pea plants (TT) with dwarf pea plants (tt). He observed that all the plants in the F1 generation were tall. When he self-pollinated the F1 plants, he found that in the F2 generation, both tall and dwarf plants appeared in a specific ratio.
(a) What was the phenotype of the F1 generation and why?
(b) What is the genotypic ratio observed in the F2 generation?
(c) (i) State the 'Law of Dominance' based on the results of this cross.
OR
(c) (ii) List any two reasons why Mendel chose pea plants for his inheritance experiments.
additional_info: Case-based Question with an internal choice.

In myopia, a person can see nearby objects clearly but cannot see distant objects distinctly. In hypermetropia, a person can see distant objects clearly but cannot see nearby objects distinctly. These defects can be corrected by using suitable spherical lenses.
(a) A person can see distant objects clearly but finds it difficult to read a book. What is this defect called?
(b) List two possible causes for this vision defect.
(c) (i) Draw a neat, labelled ray diagram to show how this defect is corrected using a suitable lens.
OR
(c) (ii) The far point of a myopic person is 80 cm in front of the eye. What is the nature and power of the lens required to enable him to see distant objects clearly?
